records.ety: [L. emolumentum, lit., a working out, fr. emoliri to move out, work out; e out + moliri to set in motion, exert one's self, fr. moles a huge, heavy mass: cf. F. molument. See Mole a mound.]

defs: The profit arising from office, employment, or labor; gain; compensation; advantage; perquisites, fees, or salary.
quotes: A long . . . enjoyment of the emoluments of office.
